# Vertical Slice and MVP Shaping

## MVP proof

The MVP should prove:
- the local world self-runs month by month
- the player can intervene through bounded commands
- family, commoner pressure, exams, trade, and grudges form one loop
- the shell feels like a clan seat, not a spreadsheet

## Good slice shape

- one region
- one playable clan
- one living monthly loop
- one shell pass through hall, lineage, desk, and notice
- one or two bounded command windows
- visible next-month consequence

## Bad slice shape

- too many systems with no consequence chain
- broad lore coverage with thin interaction
- combat layer before the hall loop feels alive
- office or warfare depth before household pressure is readable

## Cut rules

Keep:
- loop integrity
- explainable causality
- shell readability
- bounded command proof

Cut or defer:
- standalone spectacle
- excessive subsystem breadth
- deep content that does not reinforce the loop
- anything that makes MVP structurally different from later versions
